behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failures here usually occur at the git subprocess boundary (rename detection output/flags) plus parser handoff. A mocked diff/status payload can miss true git rename semantics, so System is the minimum to catch production-real failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git change categorization, with no AI/ML step (so not Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a baseline file, rename it, run the CLI exactly as shipped, and assert stdout reports 1 rename and 0 add/0 delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff behavior depends on real git subprocess output and real filesystem bytes. In-process fakes often hide encoding/stream/parsing crashes seen only with actual git binary handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no model call or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), invoke the CLI, assert process exits successfully and the binary-related change is reflected in reported counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This failure is tied to real git command behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo (exit codes/messages), which is a subprocess/system interaction. Mocking git responses can mask the exact error path. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero-exit (or defined non-fatal exit contract) and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against actual repository history, requiring real git history traversal and subprocess output fidelity. Integration stubs can validate wiring but may miss real-history edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifications across commits, run the CLI for the target range, and assert reported category counts match the ground truth built from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged state lives in real git index/worktree mechanics and subprocess queries. Mocks frequently miss index-specific behavior, so System is the minimum reliable boundary. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is git state comparison only, not AI/ML or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change sets in separate runs, execute the CLI for each mode, and assert normalized structured stdout is identical.
